steps.title: Identify the cost structure
steps.content: The first seed kit costs 18 dollars. Each seed kit after the first costs 13 dollars. If $x$ is the total number of seed kits purchased, then $(x - 1)$ seed kits are purchased at the 13-dollar rate.

steps.title: Build the equation
steps.content:
Total cost = cost of first seed kit + cost of remaining seed kits:
$$y = 18 + 13(x - 1) = 13(x - 1) + 18$$

steps.title: Verify with a test value
steps.content: For $x = 3$: $y = 13(2) + 18 = 26 + 18 = 44$. Check: first seed kit 18 dollars + two more at 13 dollars each = 18 dollars + 26 dollars = 44 dollars. Correct.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = x + 18$: Charges 1 dollar per additional seed kit instead of 13 dollars. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 13x + 18$: Charges 13 dollars for all $x$ seed kits including the first, then adds 18 dollars on top. For $x = 1$: $y = 31$, but it should be 18 dollars. ✗
  • Choice C — $y = 13(x - 1) + 18$: 18 dollars for the first seed kit and 13 dollars for each of the $(x - 1)$ additional seed kits. For $x = 1$: $y = 18$. For $x = 2$: $y = 31$. ✓
  • Choice D — $y = (x - 1) + 18$: Charges 1 dollar per additional seed kit instead of 13 dollars. ✗
C is correct.
